channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Incredible New Mod Lets You Play Fallout in Fallout
item.content:encoded:
If you’ve ever wanted to double up on your Fallout fun, now is the time. Recently, a talented modder created something amazing, a mod that allows you to play other games on your in-game Pip-Boy in Fallout 4. It takes a fair whack to get it all running smoothly, but with this mod, you can play Fallout while playing Fallout, and that just sounds beautiful.

What’s next? Perhaps we’ll be able to sit down in our safe houses in Grand Theft Auto 6, boot up the PlayStation console we know will be there, and play some Grand Theft Auto: San Andreas, or something.

Fallout 4 Mod Introduces Games to Pip-Boy

The Pip-Boy is a wondrous invention, both in and out of the Fallout universe. It allows you to manage your entire character, from skill point distribution and healing mechanics to inventory management and map traversal. It’s the hub of every modern Fallout title, and now, it’s being pushed further than ever before.

While we’ve seen some mods that tweak how the Pip-Boy works, we’ve never seen one that adds other games to the device.

On Nexus Mods, RPGKing177 uploaded a mod that adds Morrowind, the classic Elder Scrolls game, to the Pip-Boy. The mod’s description reads:

This mod streams The Elder Scrolls III: Morrowind live into the Fallout 4 Pip-Boy screen using a custom-modified build of OpenMW 0.50 — bridged directly into the game engine via shared memory and a dedicated F4SE plugin. Insert the Morrowind Holotape, open your Pip-Boy, and the rest is up to you.

Due to a repository issue, the Fallout 1 version of the mod is not available via Nexus. The Morrowind one is, but we’re here for the post-apocalyptic double-dip, right? That being said, RPGKing117 did share a video showcasing Fallout 1 working just fine via the mod:

It’s expected that you’ll be able to install the mod via Nexus soon enough; it just wasn’t available at the time of writing.

Are you going to play Fallout 1 in Fallout 4? Let us know what you think about this mod on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that EVE Online’s developer rebranded for a strange reason

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: EVE Online Dev Rebranded to Escape Chinese Communist Party
item.content:encoded:
Earlier this month, it was revealed that CCP, the Icelandic developer behind EVE Online, had been bought out by its CEO, Hilmar Veigar Pétursson, in a cash-and-crypto deal. The studio was bought out almost a decade ago by Pearl Abyss, of Crimson Desert fame, for double what it was reacquired for.

Following the sale, CCP rebranded to Fenris Creations, and in an all-new interview, the CEO has explained why they changed the face of the company after so many years, and after securing the brand back from Pearl Abyss.

The Other CCP

In a new interview with Games Industry, Hilmar Veigar Pétursson explained that the concept of security independence from Pearl Abyss had been floating around for several years. The split came amicably, resulting in a transaction worth $120 million, with $20 million of that being stumped up in cryptocurrency.

CCP, which had been a name in the gaming space for almost thirty years, no longer exists in that capacity, as Fenris Creations is now the sole identity of the Icelandic firm.

Pétursson explained why that is:

It is an interesting journey of the brand collision between Crowd Control Protections and the Chinese Communist Party.

That is a growing collision. It’s a fight I’m not going to fight. I’m not going to fight the Chinese Communist Party over who is the CCP. Obviously, the Chinese Communist Party doesn’t think of itself as the CCP. That’s a Western construct. They refer to it by a Chinese name. They’re not called the Chinese Communist Party in Iceland either; we call it the Kommúnistaflokkur Kína in Icelandic.

He went on to outline the issues the company had long experienced with the name convention.

For instance, travelling to the United States under the CCP label resulted in questionable impressions from customs agents. In the press, the company was often referred to as ‘the other CCP’.

This move enables CCP to take on a fresh look and feel, and in the words of the CEO, it allows them to more closely associate with their Icelandic heritage.

Do you think Fenris Creations is a better brand name than CCP? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Crimson Desert is getting an extraction feature

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

item.title: New Raid Mode For Pokémon TCG Pocket Possibly Leaked
item.content:encoded:
Pokémon TCG Pocket has come a long way since the beginning, and the game is almost unrecognizable with how many new features and modes have been added. A new one looks set to join the adventure.

Hopefully, you’re still enjoying Pokémon TCG Pocket’s B3 Pulsing Aura expansion—gotten any Crown cards yet?

Before we’ve even got wind of the next set, a leak has suggested that the new game mode will have ‘Raid’ qualities, and it sounds peculiar.

Pokémon TCG Pocket Could Add A Raid Mode

X user MAGIKARP has posted details on what they believe to be the next game mode addition to the free-to-play mobile game.

It’s tentatively being labeled as ‘Raid Pack,’ and it sounds…complicated.

Apparently, you and several others join forces in one lobby and open packs together. It requires stamina to do so, and you slowly unveil a special pack to get the goodies inside.

MAGIKARP also adds that the terms ‘Raid Pack,’ ‘Raid Pack Owner,’ and ‘Raid Pack Guest,’ have been discovered, giving more credence to the potential upcoming game mode.

Well, a different sort of Raid mode could be coming to Pokémon TCG Pocket. If it works, it could be a nice novelty away from the pressures of Ranked and the normal opening of Packs.

If it becomes official, rest assured that Insider Gaming will bring you all the details firsthand, and keep checking back for updates on the eventual new expansion sure to drop soon.

What do you think of the leak? Does this mode sound fun? Or is it more unusual than a Tom Jones song? Let us know your thoughts via the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more news, read about Crimson Desert getting an extraction mechanic and Resident Evil Requiem DLC being shadow dropped.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: Crimson Desert is Getting an Extraction Mechanic, But Not Like You’d Think
item.content:encoded:
Pearl Abyss has explained what’s coming in the next major Crimson Desert update, slated to arrive in just a few days. The high-level rundown of what’s inbound to the stellar open-world RPG is light on the details, but it contains some key notes as to what we can expect, including new special mounts.

There’s a note about a new extraction feature coming to Crimson Desert with that impending update, and while it made me perform a classic double-take, it’s not at all what you’d think.

Crimson Desert’s Next Major Update is Coming

Pearl Abyss has been moving mountains to evolve Crimson Desert, and in the few weeks it has been on the market, the team has released several major updates. The quality-of-life improvements have run wild, and almost everything done to make the game better has been influenced by community feedback.

In a new post on social media, Pearl Abyss explained what the next major update is looking like, and they (foolishly) featured the term ‘extraction’.

This prompted stomachs to drop everywhere, as that word is now synonymous with one of the most divisive gaming genres to ever find popularity. But it’s nothing to do with extraction shooters, that’s for damn sure.

The team wrote:

The update will be released within the next few days and will include new special mounts, an extraction feature that will allow you to recover materials used in equipment refinement, and more.

It’s an extraction mechanic that allows users to claw back some of the precious materials used when refining equipment. It’s not exactly clear how this will work, but it shouldn’t be long until the update is released and we figure all that out for ourselves.

Are you still enjoying Crimson Desert? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Sony has suffered a huge loss tied to Bungie

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.
